facebook.content_angle: Six-beat kick training — the measurable speed gain most club swimmers default away from, with a stat-backed drill breakdown showing 10%+ improvement in 50m times over four weeks
facebook.post_text:
Most swimmers are leaving a 10% speed gain completely untouched. 🚀

If you default to a two-beat kick in training, you're not alone — but you're also not fast. Most club swimmers drop to a lazy two-beat to save energy and never build the neuromuscular pattern that actually makes them quicker.

👉 Research on intermediate swimmers showed a 10.21% reduction in 50m time trial performance after just four weeks of dedicated six-beat kick training.
👉 The gain isn't fitness — it's rhythm, timing, and body rotation working together.
👉 The two-beat kick costs you balance and momentum. The six-beat kick locks them in.

Speed isn't always about the engine. Sometimes it's the wheels. ⚡

✔️ Start with a kick board — count six beats per arm cycle, not per stroke.
✔️ Progress to full freestyle, focusing on ankle flexibility and hip-driven rotation.
✔️ Four weeks of consistent drill work is all it takes to feel the difference.

Are you training six-beat or two-beat right now — and do you actually know which one you're using?
facebook.engagement_bait: Drop a 2️⃣ or 6️⃣ below — which kick pattern are you swimming with?
